sections.label: Section 1188
sections.original: τουτὶ μὲν οὐδέν πω πρὸς ἕνην τε καὶ νέαν .
sections.translation: This surely is nothing whatever to the Old and New .

sections.label: Section 1189
sections.original: ἐκεῖνος οὖν τὴν κλῆσιν ἐς δύʼ ἡμέρας
sections.translation: He therefore made the summons for two days , for the Old and New , that the deposits might be made on the first of the month .

sections.label: Section 1192
sections.original: ἵνα δὴ τί τὴν ἕνην προσέθηχʼ ;
sections.translation: Why , pray , did he add the old day ?

sections.label: Section 1192b
sections.original: ἵνʼ ὦ μέλε
sections.translation: In order , my good sir , that the defendants , being present a day before , might compromise the matter of their own accord ; but if not ,

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.original: ἕωθεν ὑπανιῷντο τῇ νουμηνία .
sections.translation: that they might be worried on the morning of the new moon .

sections.label: Section 1196
sections.original: πῶς οὐ δέχονται δῆτα τῇ νουμηνίᾳ
sections.translation: Why , then , do the magistrates not receive the deposits on the new moon , but on the Old and New ?

sections.label: Section 1198
sections.original: ὅπερ οἱ προτένθαι γὰρ δοκοῦσί μοι ποιεῖν ·
sections.translation: They seem to me to do what the forestallers do : in order that they may appreciate the deposits as soon as possible , on this account they have the first pick by one day .

sections.label: Section 1201
sections.original: εὖ γʼ · ὦ κακοδαίμονες , τί κάθησθʼ ἀβέλτεροι ,
sections.translation: Bravo ! Ye wretches , why do you sit senseless , the gain of us wise men , being blocks , ciphers , mere sheep , jars heaped together , wherefore I must sing an encomium upon myself and this my son ,

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.original: ἐπʼ εὐτυχίαισιν ᾀστέον μοὐγκώμιον .
sections.translation: on account of our good fortune .

sections.label: Section 1206
sections.original: μάκαρ ὦ Στρεψίαδες ,
sections.translation: O happy Strepsiades ! How wise you are yourself , and how excellent is the son whom you are rearing !" My friends

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.original: χοἰ δημόται
sections.translation: and fellow-tribesmen will say of me , envying me , when you prove victorious in arguing causes . But first I wish to lead you in and entertain you .

sections.label: Section 1214
sections.original: εἶτʼ ἄνδρα τῶν αὑτοῦ τι χρὴ προϊέναι ;
sections.translation: Then , ought a man to throw away any part of his own property ?

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original: οὐδέποτέ γʼ , ἀλλὰ κρεῖττον εὐθὺς ἦν τότε
sections.translation: Never ! But it were better then at once to put away blushes , rather than now to have trouble ; since I am now dragging you to be a witness , for the sake of my own money ; and further , in addition to this , I shall become an enemy to my fellow-tribesman .

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.original: ἀτὰρ οὐδέποτέ γε τὴν πατρίδα καταισχυνῶ
sections.translation: But never , while I live , will I disgrace my country , but will summon Strepsiades .

sections.label: Section 1221b
sections.original: τίς οὑτοσί ;
sections.translation: Who ’ s there ?

sections.label: Section 1222
sections.original: ἐς τὴν ἕνην τε καὶ νέαν .
sections.translation: For the Old and New .

sections.label: Section 1222b
sections.original: μαρτύρομαι ,
sections.translation: I call you to witness , that he has named it for two days . For what matter do you summon me ?

sections.label: Section 1224
sections.original: τῶν δώδεκα μνῶν , ἃς ἔλαβες ὠνούμενος
sections.translation: For the twelve minae , which you received when you were buying the dapple-gray horse .

sections.label: Section 1225b
sections.original: ἵππον ; οὐκ ἀκούετε ;
sections.translation: A horse ? Do you not hear ? I , whom you all know to hate horsemanship !

sections.label: Section 1227
sections.original: καὶ νὴ Δίʼ ἀποδώσειν γʼ ἐπώμνυς τοὺς θεούς .
sections.translation: And , by Jupiter ! You swore by the gods too , that you would repay it .

sections.label: Section 1228
sections.original: μὰ τὸν Δίʼ οὐ γάρ πω τότʼ ἐξηπίστατο
sections.translation: Ay , by Jove ! For then my Phidippides did not yet know the irrefragable argument .

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: νῦν δὲ διὰ τοῦτʼ ἔξαρνος εἶναι διανοεῖ ;
sections.translation: And do you now intend , on this account , to deny the debt ?

sections.label: Section 1231
sections.original: τί γὰρ ἄλλʼ ἂν ἀπολαύσαιμι τοῦ μαθήματος ;
sections.translation: Why , what good should I get else from his instruction ?

sections.label: Section 1232
sections.original: καὶ ταῦτʼ ἐθελήσεις ἀπομόσαι μοι τοὺς θεοὺς
sections.translation: And will you be willing to deny these upon oath of the gods ?

sections.label: Section 1233b
sections.original: τοὺς ποίους θεούς ;
sections.translation: What gods ?

sections.label: Section 1234
sections.original: τὸν Δία , τὸν Ἑρμῆν , τὸν Ποσειδῶ .
sections.translation: Jupiter , Mercury , and Neptune .

sections.label: Section 1234b
sections.original: νὴ Δία
sections.translation: Yes , by Jupiter ! And would pay down , too , a three-obol piece besides to swear .

sections.label: Section 1236
sections.original: ἀπόλοιο τοίνυν ἕνεκʼ ἀναιδείας ἔτι .
sections.translation: Then may you perish some day for your impudence !

sections.label: Section 1237
sections.original: ἁλσὶν διασμηχθεὶς ὄναιτʼ ἂν οὑτοσί .
sections.translation: This man would be the better for it if he were cleansed by rubbing with salt .

sections.label: Section 1238
sections.original: οἴμʼ ὡς καταγελᾷς .
sections.translation: Ah me , how you deride me !

sections.label: Section 1238b
sections.original: ἓξ χοᾶς χωρήσεται .
sections.translation: He will contain six choae .

sections.label: Section 1239
sections.original: οὔ τοι μὰ τὸν Δία τὸν μέγαν καὶ τοὺς θεοὺς
sections.translation: By great Jupiter and the gods , you certainly shall not do this to me with impunity !

sections.label: Section 1240b
sections.original: θαυμασίως ἥσθην θεοῖς ,
sections.translation: I like your gods amazingly ; and Jupiter , sworn by , is ridiculous to the knowing ones .

sections.label: Section 1242
sections.original: ἦ μὴν σὺ τούτων τῷ χρόνῳ δώσεις δίκην .
sections.translation: You will assuredly suffer punishment , some time or other , for this . But answer and dismiss me , whether you are going to repay me my money or not .

sections.label: Section 1244b
sections.original: ἔχε νυν ἥσυχος .
sections.translation: Keep quiet now , for I will presently answer you distinctly .

sections.label: Section 1246
sections.original: τί σοι δοκεῖ δράσειν ;
sections.translation: ( to his summons-witness ). What do you think he will do ?

sections.label: Section 1246b
sections.original: ἀποδώσειν μοι δοκεῖ .
sections.translation: I think he will pay you .

sections.label: Section 1247
sections.original: ποῦ ʼσθʼ οὗτος ἁπαιτῶν με τἀργύριον ; λέγε
sections.translation: Where is this man who asks me for his money ? Tell me what is this ?

sections.label: Section 1248b
sections.original: τοῦθʼ ὅ τι ἐστί ; κάρδοπος .
sections.translation: What is this ? A κάρδοπος .

sections.label: Section 1249
sections.original: ἔπειτʼ ἀπαιτεῖς τἀργύριον τοιοῦτος ὤν ;
sections.translation: And do you then ask me for your money , being such an ignorant person ? I would not pay , not even an obolus , to any one who called the καρδόπη κάρδοπος .

sections.label: Section 1252
sections.original: οὐκ ἄρʼ ἀποδώσεις ;
sections.translation: Then won ’ t you pay me ?

sections.label: Section 1252b
sections.original: οὐχ ὅσον γέ μʼ εἰδέναι .
sections.translation: Not , as far as I know . Will you not then pack off as fast as possible from my door ?

sections.label: Section 1254b
sections.original: ἄπειμι , καὶ τοῦτʼ ἴσθʼ ὅτι
sections.translation: I will depart ; and be assured of this , that I will make deposit against you , or may I live no longer !

sections.label: Section 1256
sections.original: καὶ προσαπολεῖς ἄρʼ αὐτὰ πρὸς ταῖς δώδεκα .
sections.translation: Then you will lose it besides , in addition to your twelve minae . And yet I do not wish you to suffer this , because you named the kardopos floolishly .

sections.label: Section 1259
sections.original: ἰώ μοί μοι .
sections.translation: Ah me ! Ah me !

sections.label: Section 1260
sections.original: ἔα .
sections.translation: Ha ! Whoever is this , who is lamenting ? Surely it was not one of Carcinus ’ deities that spoke .

sections.label: Section 1262
sections.original: τί δʼ ὅστις εἰμὶ τοῦτο βούλεσθʼ εἰδέναι ;
sections.translation: But why do you wish to know this , who I am ?- A miserable man .

sections.label: Section 1263b
sections.original: κατὰ σεαυτόν νυν τρέπου .
sections.translation: Then follow your own path .

sections.label: Section 1264
sections.original: ὦ σκληρὲ δαῖμον , ὦ τύχαι θραυσάντυγες
sections.translation: O harsh fortune ! O Fates , breaking the wheels of my horses ! O Pallas , how you have destroyed me !

sections.label: Section 1266
sections.original: τί δαί σε Τληπόλεμός ποτʼ εἴργασται κακόν ;
sections.translation: What evil , pray , has Tlepolemus ever done you ?

sections.label: Section 1267
sections.original: μὴ σκῶπτέ μʼ ὦ τᾶν , ἀλλά μοι τὰ χρήματα
sections.translation: Do not jeer me , my friend ; but order your son to pay me the money which he received ; especially as I have been unfortunate .

sections.label: Section 1270
sections.original: τὰ ποῖα ταῦτα χρήμαθʼ ;
sections.translation: What money is this ?

sections.label: Section 1270b
sections.original: ἁδανείσατο .
sections.translation: That which he borrowed .

sections.label: Section 1271
sections.original: κακῶς ἄρʼ ὄντως εἶχες , ὥς γʼ ἐμοὶ δοκεῖς .
sections.translation: Then you were really unlucky , as I think .

sections.label: Section 1272
sections.original: ἵππους ἐλαύνων ἐξέπεσον νὴ τοὺς θεούς .
sections.translation: By the gods , I fell while driving my horses .

sections.label: Section 1273
sections.original: τί δῆτα ληρεῖς ὥσπερ ἀπʼ ὄνου καταπεσών ;
sections.translation: Why , pray , do you talk nonsense , as if you had fallen from an ass ?

sections.label: Section 1274
sections.original: ληρῶ , τὰ χρήματʼ ἀπολαβεῖν εἰ βούλομαι ;
sections.translation: Do I talk nonsense if I wish to recover my money ?

sections.label: Section 1275
sections.original: οὐκ ἔσθʼ ὅπως σύ γʼ αὐτὸς ὑγιαίνεις .
sections.translation: You can ’ t be in your senses yourself .

sections.label: Section 1275b
sections.original: τί δαί ;
sections.translation: Why , pray ?

sections.label: Section 1276
sections.original: τὸν ἐγκέφαλον ὥσπερ σεσεῖσθαί μοι δοκεῖς .
sections.translation: You appear to me to have had your brains shaken as it were .
